learning_outcomes:
HIST 1.  |  Major Programme:
At
the end of the course of their studies, students will have:
(1)  |  gained
a solid training of empirical methods for the study of history;
(2)  |  mastered
knowledge of diversified areas of historical study (national history,
comparative history and public history);
(3)  |  developed
critical thinking on diversified areas of historical study;
(4)  |  produced
creative work and conduct scholarly research;
(5)  |  learnt
to communicate effectively in academic discourse and the professional
workplace, including making effective written and oral presentations using
appropriate tool and technology;
(6)  |  developed
the ability to apply the knowledge and skills of historical study in
different context after graduation; and
(7)  |  developed
the ability and habit to continue building upon their knowledge and skills
after graduation, with an interest of learning as a lifelong process.
2.  |  History
and English Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have acquired:
(1)  |  command
of appropriate academic and creative English language skills; (K)
(2)  |  progress
in humanities research, particularly linguistic and historical research; (K)
(3)  |  deepened
understanding of both English and Chinese culture and history; (K)
(4)  |  critical
and creative thinking; (S)
(5)  |  communication
skills and computer literacy; (S)
(6)  |  self-managed
learning, adaptability and problem solving skills; (S)
(7)  |  ability
to appreciate global cultural diversity, exchanges and mutual dependence; (V)
(8)  |  ability
to value and appreciate the beauty of language and culture and the pleasure
of humanities research; and (V)
(9)  |  ability
to consolidate cosmopolitan identity and cultural openness of Hong Kong. (V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude
outcomes
3.  |  History
and Japanese Studies Double Major Programme:
Upon completing the Programme, students will have acquired:
(1)  |  command
of Japanese language; (K)
(2)  |  progress
in humanities research, particularly historical research and Japanese Studies; (K)
(3)  |  deepened
understanding of both Japanese and Chinese culture and history; (K)
(4)  |  critical
and creative thinking; (S)
(5)  |  communication
skills and computer literacy; (S)
(6)  |  self-managed
learning, adaptability and problem solving skills; (S)
(7)  |  ability
to appreciate East Asian cultural diversity, exchanges and mutual dependence; (V)
(8)  |  ability
to value and appreciate the beauty of language and culture and the joy of
humanities research; and (V)
(9)  |  ability
to help consolidate cosmopolitan identity and cultural openness of East Asia.
(V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and
attitude outcomes
4.  |  Minor
Programme:
(i)  |  History
(ii)  |  Cultural
Heritage Studies
Upon
completion of the Minor Programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired knowledge
of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a suitable
level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects or to pursue
a related career.
(iii)  |  Public History
Upon completion of this Minor Programme,
students will:
•  |  have acquired knowledge of the origin,
development, and major debates in the field of public history. (K)
•  |  have developed an understanding of the
presentation skills of public history and the ability to undertake public
history projects. (S)
•  |  have developed an understanding of
ethics in the practice of public history, as well as the value of history and
culture in building personal philosophy of life. (V)
K
= Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude
outcomes

Students
are required to complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:

Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 90 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package  |  9
HIST1000#, JASP1090# and any one
course from: ANTH1020, ARTS1004, 1005, 1010, BMBL1001,
CHES1100, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400[CUMT1000], ENGE1000,
FAAS1900, HIST1700# or 1701#, LING1000, MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000,
TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
1st Major: History HIST1001#, 1002#  |  6
2nd Major: Japanese
Studies[a] JASP1470, 1480, 2010#, 2030#, 2470,
2480  |  24
3.  |  Elective Courses:
1st Major: History Students are required to complete 30
units of HIST courses, including at least 6 units from: HIST2002, 2005, 2006, 2007  |  30
2nd Major: Japanese
Studies[b] Students are required to complete 15
units of JASP courses from (a) Non-language courses and (b) Language
courses as listed below, with at least 6 units of 2000 or above level
courses from (a) Non-language courses and at least 3 units of 4000 level
courses from (b) Language courses. (a) Non-language
Courses: JASP1120, 1140,
1160, 1180, 1190, 2100, 2200, 2400, 2520, 2600, 2720, 2820, 2910, 2920,
3110, 3120, 3210, 3430, 3470, 3480, 3500, 3700, 3800, 3900, 4000, 4100, 4110, 4410, ANTH2327, 2350,
LING1000[c], 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 3101, 3201, 3204, MGNT1010, 1020,
4530, SOCI2103 (b) Language
Courses: JASP1490, 2000, 3180[d], 3200[d], 3310[d], 3320[d], 3330[d], 3340[d], 3350[d],
3360[d], 3380[d], 3390[d], 3600[d], 4180,4350, 4380, 4390, 4440, 4510,
4520  |  15
4.  |  Research Component/ Capstone Course/
Final Year Project:  |  6
HIST4811, 4812
Total:  |  90
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  HIST courses at 2000 and above level
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Students who
have prior knowledge of Japanese before admission may be exempted from
taking JASP1470, 1480, 2470 and 2480. They should seek the advice of
Department of Japanese Studies to take substitute courses in accordance
with their academic qualifications as follows:
[i] Students
who passed placement test and exempted from taking any or all required
language course(s) (JASP1470, 1480, 2470 and/or 2480) are required to
take corresponding units of Language courses at 3000 or 4000 level
specified under 3(b) above, to substitute for the relevant course(s).
These substitute courses cannot be double-counted to fulfill the required
elective of 3 units of 4000 level language course.
[ii] Students
who possess Japanese Language Proficiency Test (N2) qualification when
admitted are required to take 18 units of courses from 3(a) and/or 3(b)
above, with at least 12 units of Language Courses at 3000 or 4000 level,
to substitute for the 4 relevant courses. These substitute courses cannot
be double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000
level language course.
[iii] Students
who possess Japanese Language Proficiency Test (N1) qualification when
admitted are required to take 18 units of courses from 3(a) and/or 3(b)
above, with at least 9 units of Language Courses at 4000 level, to
substitute for the 4 relevant courses. These substitute courses cannot be
double-counted to fulfill the required elective of 3 units of 4000 level
language course.
[iv]
Native Japanese students are required to take 18 units of courses from
3(a) and/or 3(b) above, with or without any Language Courses, to
substitute for the 4 relevant courses. If they choose to take Language
Courses, they are allowed to take Language Courses at 4000 level only,
subject to the approval of the course teacher. If they choose to take
non-language courses, they should take at least 12 units of elective
courses at 2000 or above level.
[b]  |  Subject to Department’s approval, native speaker
of Japanese can be exempted from the fulfillment of one JASP 4000 level
language course, and take 3 units of JASP course as a substitution.
[c]  |  LING1000 which counts towards the fulfillment of
the requirements specified in 1 Faculty Package cannot be used for the
fulfillment of the requirements specified in 3(a) Non-language Courses.
[d]  |  These courses are only offered to students
before their resident study in Japan.
[ ]  |  Old course code used in 2024-25 and before.

Minor Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of courses as follows:

HIST1000
2.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  HIST1001 or 1002  |  3
(b)  |  4 HIST courses excluding HIST1001
and 1002, with at least 2 courses at 3000 or above level  |  12
Total:  |  18

Minor Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of courses with at least 2
courses at 3000 or above level as follows:
Units

HIST1700

3406, 3505, 4142, 4500, 4502, 4600, 4700, 4701, 4910  |  9 3

Note:

students who major in History, History and English Double Major

Minor Programme Requirement
